data.title: Posco Future’s energy materials unit swings to loss
data.description:
Posco Future M Co. (Gyeongsang, South Korea) has reported a 47% year-over-year decline in second-quarter revenue to 506 billion South Korean won ($230 million) for the company’s energy materials business, as per Chemweek.

The unit posted an operating loss of 8.1 billion won, compared with an operating profit of 1.3 billion won in the corresponding period of the previous year.In the cathode subsector, revenue fell 50% year over year to 273.1 billion won. There was a decrease in sales of high-Ni cathode materials on a quarter-over-quarter basis.In the anode subsector, Posco Future M recorded lower sales of 42.3 billion won, down 16% year over year. Sales of NG anodes increased sequentially, primarily driven by demand from overseas customers.Despite the increase in NG anode sales, overall profitability in the cathode subsector deteriorated. This decline was primarily due to lower operating rates. Additionally, the company faced inventory valuation losses.Posco’s other business is basic materials, including refractories, furnace maintenance/construction and quicklime/chemicals.

mrchub.com

data.title: German Business Sentiment Rises Modestly, Misses Expectations
data.description:
The Ifo Business Climate Index for Germany edged up to 88.6 in July 2025, from 88.4 in June, marking the highest reading since May 2024.

However, the result came in slightly below market expectations of 89.0, suggesting that optimism remains cautious.

The improvement was driven by a marginal uptick in both current assessments (86.5 vs 86.2) and business expectations (90.7 vs 90.6), indicating firms are becoming slightly less pessimistic about economic conditions.

data.title: Brigade Hotel Ventures' India IPO fully subscribed on second day of bids
data.description:
Brigade Hotel Ventures' 7.6-billion-rupee ($88 million) initial public offering was fully subscribed on the second day of bidding on Friday, as investor optimism about India's burgeoning hospitality sector strengthens.

Total bids matched the shares on offer, a fresh issue, exchange data showed as of 1:28 p.m. local time.

Shares of the hotel operator, which is aiming for a valuation of around $397 million, are slated to list on July 31, Thursday.

India's hotel operators are cashing in on the surge in travel, as both business and leisure trips rebound across the country. Fueling the momentum is a wave of wealthy Indians spending big on luxury, not just on stays, but on cars, apartments, designer watches and even upscale bathrooms.

Hospitality firms such as ITC Hotels and Leela-owner Schloss Bangalore have made a beeline for public markets over the past two years, betting on sustained demand for premium experiences.

Brigade Hotel Ventures first filed for an IPO in October with an offer size of 9 billion, with the trimmed IPO size coming after a partial debt repayment and pre-IPO fundraises.

The firm runs nine hotels across five Indian cities, partnering with Marriott , Accor and IHG in the mid-to-upscale segments.

($1 = 86.5150 Indian rupees)

data.title: China's Plan to Curb Pharma Price War Bodes Well for Sector — Market Talk
data.description: China's plan to rectify a price war in the pharma industry should bode well for the sector, DBS says in a note. In future selections of medical-products suppliers for public hospitals, the lowest bid might not win, China's National Healthcare Security Administration says. DBS thinks this move alleviates the largest risk to China's pharma sector--price cuts. Chinese pharma stocks could have further room to grow as the valuation isn't high from a historical perspective, it says. In view of better industry environment, the market could raise pharma companies' 2H sales and earnings forecasts, it adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: German Business Sentiment Edges Higher Despite Tariff Uncertainty
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

German business confidence inched up this month, a signal of resilience against U.S. tariffs, although with high uncertainty keeping any upswing in check.

The Ifo Institute said Friday that its business-climate index rose to 88.6 in July from 88.4 in June, a little weaker than the 89.0 expected by economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal.

"The upturn in the German economy remains sluggish," Ifo President Clemens Fuest said.

Companies were slightly more satisfied with current business, though their expectations for future business remained largely unchanged, he added.

Indexes for the manufacturing and construction industries rose in July, but they declined for the services and trade sectors, Ifo said.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 4 AM ET: Eni Lifts Cash-Flow Guidance, Confirms $1.8 Billion Buyback Despite Headwinds | Trump ...
data.description:
Eni Lifts Cash-Flow Guidance, Confirms $1.8 Billion Buyback Despite Headwinds

The company confirmed its planned shareholder returns for 2025 of a 5% dividend increase and a buyback program.

----

Trump Administration Greenlights Chevron to Resume Pumping Oil in Venezuela

The decision marks a softening in the White House's approach to the South American country.

----

Oil Snaps Losing Streak on Trade-Deal Optimism

Oil rose for the first time in five sessions with help from expectations of the U.S. reaching a trade deal with the EU, while continuing to trade in a tight range.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Edge Higher

U.S. natural gas futures eked out a small gain after the EIA reported a below-normal inventory build of 23 Bcf for last week, leaving inventories at 3,075 Bcf or 171 Bcf above the five-year average.

----

Private Equity Gets Bullish on Natural Gas-Fired Power Plants

Deals involving the assets totaled $49 billion during the first six months of the year.

----

TotalEnergies Maintains Buyback Pace Despite Profit Drop, Higher Debt

The energy major said it would keep its $2 billion quarterly buyback even as lower oil-and-gas prices weighed on its earnings.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil, Centrica and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories declined by 3.2 million barrels as domestic production and imports fell while exports picked up.

----

GE Vernova Stock Soared on Earnings Beat. Wall Street Loves the Power Firm.

The power company posted strong second-quarter earnings, and raised full-year financial guidance.

----

Equinor Takes Near $1 Billion Hit From Trump Flip-Flop on Offshore Wind

Norwegian energy giant said stalling of construction of its Empire Wind project off the New York coast and an issue with a lease cost the company $955 million.

----

Halliburton Sees Softer Oilfield Services Market as Profit, Revenue Slide

Halliburton expects the oilfield services market will be softer than previously thought after logging lower second-quarter profit and revenue.

----

U.K. Government Approves $51 Billion Sizewell C Nuclear Power Plant

The government said it will take an initial 44.9% stake in the 3.2 gigawatt nuclear power station in southeast England, becoming the project's biggest shareholder.

----

How China Curbed Its Oil Addiction-and Blunted a U.S. Pressure Point

The Chinese government is boosting domestic production and the EV industry in the name of national security. There are 14 million chargers nationwide.

data.title: Türkiye imposes duties on packaging film from China, Egypt and Russia
data.description:
Turkiye's Trade Ministry on Wednesday imposed definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of stretchable plastic film used in packaging—technically known as biaxially oriented polypropylene (BOPP) film—from China, Egypt, and Russia, following the outcome of a trade investigation, as per Turkiyetoday.According to the ministry's decision published in the Official Gazette, an anti-dumping investigation into imports of the packaging film from the three countries concluded that the products are sold below their fair market value.The investigation, launched by the ministry in February 2024 following complaints from Turkish producers, finds that these imports cause material injury to Turkiye’s domestic manufacturers by undercutting prices.

As a result of the findings, the ministry decided to implement anti-dumping tariffs on the imported plastic film. For products from China and Egypt, the duty rates will vary by company, ranging between 12.85% and 62.94% of the product’s cost, insurance, and freight (CIF) value. For imports from Russia, a flat rate of 47.14% will apply.The decision aims to restore fair competition in the local market and shield domestic producers from the effects of unfair foreign pricing.

Biaxially Oriented Polypropylene (BOPP) film is a type of plastic film widely used in the packaging industry. It is made by stretching polypropylene—a common plastic resin—in both the machine and transverse directions, which improves its strength, clarity, and barrier properties.BOPP film is commonly used for food packaging, labels, adhesive tapes, and gift wraps due to its lightweight, moisture resistance, and ability to be printed on easily.mrchub.com

data.title: Volkswagen CFO: Sales Guidance Cut Due to Demand Destruction in U.S. From Tariffs
Rightmove's Results Prove Resilience Despite Backdrop Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Rightmove's 1H shows its incredible resilience in the face of an uncertain market backdrop, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. The planning delays faced by housebuilders have done little to dent their spending on the property website, RBC says, as average revenue per advertiser grew by 105 pounds for agencies and by 153 pounds in its new-homes division. Although homebuyers hold out for lower mortgage rates, estate agents look keen to increase their spending on the company, RBC says. "Whilst no company can guarantee certainty of results, Rightmove gets closer than most," the analysts say. Shares are down 1% at 786.80 pence and are up 38.5% over the past 12 months.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: European Gas Prices Poised for Weekly Loss Amid Ample Supplies — Market Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices are poised for a weekly drop of more than 4%, pressured by ample supplies as EU members continue to refill storage facilities ahead of the winter. Inventory levels have increased by an average of 0.28% daily, according to BMI, putting the 90% refilling target within reach before the original Nov. 1 deadline. A 20% drop in Chinese LNG imports this year has eased competition for cargoes, allowing European buyers to secure more deliveries. "China's Unipec sold several LNG cargoes overseas this week, indicating domestic demand is subdued," ANZ Research analysts say. "Forecasts of heavy rain over the northern and southwestern parts of China may further ease demand." In early trade, the benchmark Dutch TTF contract falls 0.8% to 32.55 euros a megawatt hour. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Three workers rescued after being trapped at Newmont's Red Chris mine in Canada
data.description:
Three workers from Hy-Tech Drilling at Newmont's Red Chris mine project were safely brought to the surface following a fall of ground incident, the company said on Friday.

Newmont added that it will conduct a comprehensive investigation into the incident.

The workers were trapped underground at the mine on Wednesday, resulting in suspension of operations at the Red Chris gold and copper mine in British Columbia.

data.title: MC: LVMH Stock Goes Out of Fashion as Luxury Powerhouse Profit Dives 15% to €9.01 Billion
data.description:
Key points:
  • LVMH sales, profit miss marks
  • Operating profit drops 15%
  • Shares down 26% on year

House of Bernard Arnault posted its second quarter in a row where sales can’t reach estimates. What does this say about the stock and the industry?

👜 Luxury Loses Its Shine

  • LVMH stock dropped another 2% early Friday, adding to Thursday’s 2% post-earnings slide. The world’s largest luxury conglomerate reported a 15% drop in first-half operating profit to €9.01 billion and its second consecutive quarterly sales miss.
  • Second-quarter revenue came in at €19.5 billion, falling 4% from a year ago and just shy of analyst expectations of €19.6 billion. It’s the fifth miss in six quarters — not a great trend for the Paris-listed company once seen as the gold standard in premium performance.
  • The stock is now down 26% year-to-date, and more than 50% off its 2023 peak valuation north of $500 billion. For CEO Bernard Arnault’s empire, the question is no longer “how high can it go?” but “where’s the floor?”

📉 Soft Sales, Fading Glamor

  • Luxury demand is cooling, especially in previously bulletproof markets like China and the US. While LVMH didn’t blame a specific geography, the industry-wide slowdown has made it clear: the post-COVID luxury boom is over.
  • First-half sales landed at €39.81 billion, a whisker below the €39.87 billion consensus. Net income came in at €5.7 billion, below the expected €5.9 billion.
  • For a company used to topping estimates with ease, this kind of sustained underperformance is rather unusual — and for investors, it’s a wake-up call that even the bling has a ceiling.

🔍 What It Says About the Industry

  • LVMH owns titans like Dior, Louis Vuitton, Fendi, and Moët, making it a proxy for the broader high-end market. If this house of luxury is struggling, so is the industry — especially at the ultra-premium tier.
  • With sluggish global growth, sticky inflation, and a more cautious high-income consumer, the sector is facing its first real test in years. Shoppers aren’t necessarily trading down — but they’re pausing, and that pause is hitting earnings.
  • The good news? LVMH is still profitable. The bad news? It’s increasingly priced like a growth stock, not a value one — and five out of six misses don’t make for a great growth story.

data.title: German business sentiment rises less than expected in July, Ifo survey finds
data.description:
German business morale improved less than expected in July, a survey showed on Friday, as companies were more satisfied with current business conditions.

The Ifo institute said its business climate index rose to 88.6 in July from 88.4 in June. Analysts polled by Reuters had forecast a rise in the reading to 89.0.

"The upturn in the German economy remains anaemic," Ifo president Clemens Fuest said.

While the current conditions index modestly rose to 86.5 from 86.2 in the previous month, the business expectations index remained virtually unchanged.

data.title: NatWest Is in a Sweet Spot — Market Talk
data.description: NatWest's quarterly print shows that the bank is in a sweet spot after the government returned it to private ownership, Interactive Investor says in a market comment. The U.K. lender is the investor favorite in the sector and its results justify that optimism, analyst Richard Hunter writes. "It remains to be seen whether this new-found freedom will enable a more aggressive acquisition policy," he adds. The bank's significant cash generation brings up the dilemma of whether to boost shareholder returns, invest heavily in the business or pursue an acquisition, he says. The group also raised its returns and income guidance for the year and announced a 750 million pound share buyback, which has been well-received by investors. Shares rise 2% to 513 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Newmont Says Three HY-Tech Drilling Workers At The Red Chris Mine Project, Were Safely Brought To Surface Following A Fall Of Ground Incident
data.description:
Imperial Metals Corp :

  • NEWMONT- THREE HY-TECH DRILLING WORKERS AT THE RED CHRIS MINE PROJECT, WERE SAFELY BROUGHT TO SURFACE FOLLOWING A FALL OF GROUND INCIDENT

  • NEWMONT - WE WILL CONDUCT A COMPREHENSIVE INVESTIGATION INTO THE INCIDENT

data.title: REG - Whitbread PLC - Block listing Interim Review
data.description:
RNS Number : 6040S Whitbread PLC 25 July 2025  

BLOCK LISTING SIX MONTHLY RETURN

Information provided on this form must be typed or printed electronically and provided to an ris.

Date: 25 JULY 2025

Name of applicant:

WHITBREAD PLC

Name of scheme:

WHITBREAD SAVINGS RELATED SHARE OPTION SCHEME

Period of return:

From:

25 JANUARY 2025

To:

24 JULY 2025

Balance of unallotted securities under scheme(s) from previous return:

150,550

Plus:  The amount by which the block scheme(s) has been increased since the date of the last return (if any increase has been applied for):

Less:  Number of securities issued/allotted under scheme(s) during period (see LR3.5.7G):

122,213

Equals:  Balance under scheme(s) not yet issued/allotted at end of period:

28,337

Name of contact:

DAREN LOWRY, DIRECTOR OF SECRETARIAT & INSURANCE

Telephone number of contact:

01582 889346
